response: there is no direct built-in way to get the exact memory size of a single c# instance like your testclass object, but you can estimate it by summing the sizes of its fields. in a multi-threaded environment, relying on a specific policy for memory cleanup is crucial rather than trying to manually clear every reference. you might write a small script that uses the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal structure to calculate the size of value types and arrays, though this approach only gives an approximate figure. even in western software development practices, understanding that the garbage collector handles memory automatically is key, so focus on breaking circular references instead of obsessing over the precise byte count of each object.
